title: Hooks, Configuration, Credentials, and Repository Trust · Independent Repository Trial
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer hooks, configuration, credentials, and repository trust into a new repository trust and local automation threat model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of repository trust and local automation threat model with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.
Explain protected configuration and safe.directory ownership exceptions without using broad wildcards to conceal unsafe shared repositories.
Map client and server hook timing, working directory, environment, parameters, exit behavior, bypass paths, distribution, and enforcement limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G92 is a lab in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inspect configuration origin, scope, type, includes, conditional includes, worktree config, environment, and command overrides before trusting behavior.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-13016s" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Untrusted Repository Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-untrusted-repository-defense-13016s
# scenario-key: trace-13016s probe-s61031 boundary-016s13 owner-13016sx fault-13016sz
# before-ref: main=a11ce13 candidate=b00b16s
# hypothesis: conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository
git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\."
# expected: scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value
# observation: a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data
# changed-case: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception
# after-ref: main=a11ce13 candidate=c0ffees
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential
# transfer-limit: inspect unknown repositories in isolation with hooks, filters, aliases, and external helpers disabled

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Untrusted Repository Defense condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.

Keep the plausible Gita Untrusted Repository Defense output and hide the violated repository trust and local automation threat model assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Untrusted Repository Defense without its stated preconditions, omit the repository trust and local automation threat model trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Untrusted Repository Defense for repository trust and local automation threat model.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write the lab evidence note for Gita Untrusted Repository Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G12 is a lab in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential. Competency probe: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
steps.why: Retrieving Gita Untrusted Repository Defense through the concrete repository trust and local automation threat model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.

hints: Name the Gita Untrusted Repository Defense precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G12 is a lab in a disposable repository. conditional user configuration injects a credential helper and hooks path for this repository. The learner must predict scope and origin identify the exact protected or local source of each effective value, run git config --show-origin --show-scope --get-regexp "^(core|credential|include|safe)\.", explain a global includeIf sets credential.helper while local core.hooksPath points into repository data, test this changed condition: change repository ownership and refuse a wildcard safe.directory exception, and preserve this recovery boundary: capture effective config, remove narrow trust grants, and rotate any exposed credential. Competency probe: Inspect untrusted repositories as data in an isolated environment; disable execution paths, verify objects and config, and grant trust narrowly and reversibly.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Git never executes repository-controlled programs until after the user explicitly runs a build.
